content:
Hello. I was watching the latest presentation from @Miguel Ojeda and @Wedson Almeida Filho about issues with Rust for Rust for Linux project (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fVEeqo40IyQ) and thought if something similar to placement new might help with issues Wedson was describing with pin ergonomics.
Would some modification of placement by return RFC (https://github.com/rust-lang/rfcs/pull/2884) help in any way?
Warning: I know about placement new only superficially, so I have no idea if I am being helpful or not.

I think it could help if it covered the case where the return value has a local binding in the function. This example is taken from the rendered RFC:

// This function may or may not copy the array when it returns.
fn bad() -> [i32; 1_000_000] {
    let mut arr = [0; 1_000_000];
    for i in 0..1_000_000 {
        arr[i] = i;
    }
    arr
}

It explicitly says that it may not work, but this is the sort of thing we'd need to help with pinning because we need to pass a pointer to mutex_init, which is written in C.
